message:
test(survival): pin ActivityProfile::new composition on both-NaN inputs (ft-icreu)

ft-icreu (f848e8a0) added two independent `if is_nan` branches to
ActivityProfile::new — one for alpha, one for default_activity —
each snapping to a safe default before the shared clamp. Each branch
has its own dedicated construction test in the existing suite, but
the _composition_ of the two branches was not pinned: a regression
that silently coupled the two (for example, an accidental else-if
refactor that only ran the second branch when the first didn't fire)
would pass the per-branch tests while still leaving one path NaN.

This test feeds f64::NAN to both alpha AND default_activity
simultaneously and asserts:

1. All 24 hourly_ewma buckets predict_hour to 0.5 (the snap default
   for NaN default_activity), with |v - 0.5| < EPSILON. Pre-fix
   without both branches, even one bucket would be NaN and the
   assertion would fire on the first failing hour.
2. After two EWMA updates with finite samples (0.2 then 0.8), the
   blended predict_hour is finite and in [0, 1]. This exercises
   `alpha.mul_add(sample, (1.0 - alpha) * prev)` — if alpha is still
   NaN (either because the branch didn't fire or a regression
   removed it), the result would be NaN and both assertions fail.
3. The in-range check (`(0.0..=1.0).contains(&blended)`) also
   implicitly pins the clamp chain running AFTER the two NaN snaps,
   so the full normalization path is exercised end-to-end.

Pure test addition; no production code touched. Sits next to the
existing `ft_26k3h_*` and construction-time ft-icreu tests in the
survival::tests module.
